title: zeltnera wigginsii
intro: Zeltnera wigginsii, also known as Wiggins' Indian phlox or Wiggins' zeltnera, is a rare species of flowering plant in the gentian family, characterized by its delicate, pink to purple flowers and narrow, pointed leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Zeltnera wigginsii are said to include reducing inflammation, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
